name: The Twelve
description: Shadowy organization of twelve powerful men who convene at Palazzo Falconieri. They conduct discussions in Russian and English, control capital flows, and commission assassinations through formal voting procedures using antique ivory fish tokens (red for death, yellow to spare). Villanelle works for them.

name: Cosa Nostra
description: The Sicilian Mafia. The overarching structure within which clans such as the Greci and Matteo families operate.

name: 'Ndrangheta
description: Criminal organization from the southern Italian region of Calabria. Until eighteen months before the story begins, they were the principal European distribution partners of the Mexican drug cartels for cocaine. They lost this role to the Greci clan.

name: Greci clan
description: Resurgent Sicilian Mafia clan led by Salvatore Greco. Allied with the Messini clan. The richest, most powerful, and most feared Sicilian clan. They control the Palermo drug trade through an alliance with Mexican cartels, with enterprises worth an estimated $20-30 billion.

name: Matteo family
description: Rival Sicilian Mafia clan. A member of this family murdered Salvatore Greco's father in the 1990s. Salvatore subsequently hunted down and killed all surviving Mattei.

name: Messini clan
description: Sicilian Mafia clan, associates of the Greci. Part of the most powerful alliance in Sicily.

name: Brothers' Circle
description: One of the most violent and ruthless criminal organizations in Russia. Responsible for the death of Boris Vorontsov (Villanelle's father). Villanelle killed three of their foot soldiers in retaliation.

name: Corriere della Sera
description: Italian newspaper. Bruno De Santis works as a journalist there.

name: Special Boat Service
description: British special forces unit (Royal Navy). Frank was a former SBS instructor.

name: Société Générale
description: French multinational bank. Villanelle holds a current account there, funded through a day-trading cover identity.

name: Polizia di Stato
description: Italian national civil police force. Agent Paolo Vella is based in the Palermo division.

name: MI5
description: British domestic security service, headquartered at Thames House on Millbank. Eve works in the P3 section.

name: P3
description: Subsection of the Joint Services Analysis Group within MI5. Assesses threats to high-risk UK visitors and liaises with the Metropolitan Police for protection.

name: Metropolitan Police
description: London's police force. SO1 (Protection Command) and SO15 (Counter Terrorism) branches are involved in protection operations.

name: SO15
description: Counter-terrorist Command of the Metropolitan Police in London.

name: DIA
description: Direzione Investigativa Antimafia. Italian law enforcement agency specialising in investigation of organised crime and Mafia-related activities.

name: GCHQ
description: UK signals intelligence agency based in Cheltenham. Bill Tregaron was seconded here.

name: Eurasia UK
description: Organization that arranged Viktor Kedrin's visit to the UK. Promotes pro-Eurasian ideology centred on mystical bonds between Europe and Russia and opposition to American expansionism.

name: FSB
description: Russian Federal Security Service. Used Maria Golovkina for overseas assassinations. Shares intelligence with Western services on the HST list.

name: DGSE
description: Direction Generale de la Securite Exterieure. France's external intelligence service.

name: E Squadron
description: British Special Forces action team. According to Konstantin, they handle deniable operations; if British intelligence identifies Villanelle, an E Squadron visit and cremation in a municipal waste incinerator is the expected outcome.

name: SO1
description: Protection Command, Metropolitan Police. Specialist unit providing close protection for high-profile individuals.

name: MI6
description: British foreign intelligence service headquartered at Vauxhall Cross. Richard Edwards heads the Russia desk.

name: White Dragon crew
description: Elite cyber-warfare unit of the Chinese People's Liberation Army, operating from Datong Road in Shanghai's Pudong district. Led by Lt. Col. Zhang Lei (codename FatPanda). Has conducted targeted attacks against over 150 military and corporate targets in the US, Russia and Belarus over eight years.

name: People's Liberation Army
description: Chinese military forces. Zhang Lei serves as a lieutenant colonel in PLA cyber-warfare operations.

name: Ministry of State Security
description: Chinese intelligence and security agency. Jin Qiang is an officer.

name: Triads
description: Chinese organised crime syndicates.

name: North Star
description: Cover company used by the Twelve for logistics. Its Learjet bears the North Star insignia, and operatives carry reception passes identifying them as employees.

name: Kazan Military Academy
description: Military academy in Kazan, Russia.

name: D4 Branch
description: MI5 division responsible for counter-espionage against Russia and China.

name: Zoloty Bratstvo
description: Mafia gang based in Odessa, headed by Rinat Yevtukh. Operates under SVR control.

name: SVR
description: Russian foreign intelligence service (Sluzhba Vneshney Razvedki). Maintains its own operations and controls the Zoloty Bratstvo gang in Odessa.

name: GIGN
description: France's elite counter-terrorism unit (Groupe d'Intervention de la Gendarmerie Nationale). Villanelle used the cover identity Sylvie Dazat, purportedly on secondment from GIGN, while training at Fort Bragg.

name: Sverdlovsk-Futura Group
description: Moscow-registered private security company (ChVK) with a turnover of approximately $170 million. Handles security for hospitals, airports, gas pipelines, and military adviser contracts. Tony Kent is a partner. Also owns the Knock Tom marine platform off the Essex coast. Its offshore arm SF12 is registered in the British Virgin Islands for deniable operations.

name: AISE
description: Italian external intelligence agency. Alerted the Venice police to Yevtukh's presence and criminal background in the city.

name: Nouvelle Droite
description: French far-right party led by Magali Le Meur, advocating pan-European nationalism. Le Meur is regarded as the future of the country's far-right tendency.

name: Lapsit Exillis
description: Organization founded by Leonardo Venturi, described on its website as an initiatory guild for aristocrats of the spirit. Its inspiration is esoteric and linked to the Grail legend.

name: Daughters of Odin
description: Finnish far-right organization led by Inka Järvi.

name: UK Patriots Party
description: British far-right party led by Richard Baggot.

name: Panzerdämmerung
description: Dark music band associated with the far-right circuit. Three members: Petra Voss (vocals and red Fender Stratocaster), Klaus Lorenz (bass guitar), and Peter Lorenz (drums). Their music features songs about loss, forgotten rituals, and the death of tradition, counterpointed by recorded political speeches, building to devastating drum crescendos. Outfitted in black uniforms with a military edge; perform at the Felsnadel torchlit ceremony.

name: SF12
description: Company registered in the British Virgin Islands, serving as the offshore and deniable arm of the Sverdlovsk-Futura Group. Tony Kent is a partner. Under Russian law, a private military company registered abroad falls outside the legal responsibility of the Russian parliament, allowing deniable operations.

name: Directorate S
description: The espionage and agent-handling division of the SVR (Russian foreign intelligence). Its Operations Department is the Russian equivalent of the British E Squadron: special forces for deniable and deep-cover operations overseas. Konstantin Orlov formerly headed it.

name: Tass
description: Russian state news agency.

name: Gazprom
description: Russian natural gas energy conglomerate.

name: Golden Brotherhood
description: Criminal outfit based in Odessa, headed by Rinat Yevtukh. Involved in sex trafficking, people-smuggling, and drugs. Ukrainian police have Yevtukh down for at least a dozen murders but have never been able to get anyone to testify against him.

name: Prekrasnaya Nevesta
description: Russian bridal gown company with a large industrial warehouse in St Petersburg. The warehouse serves as a depot where shipping containers are unloaded. Dasha Kvariani is employed by the owner as head of security.

name: Kupchino Bratva
description: Russian criminal organization controlling the heroin trade in St Petersburg. Led by a Pakhan with a hierarchical structure of brigadiers and soldiers. Dasha Kvariani inherited her brigade from her father Gennadi. Women holding rank is almost unheard of in the gangs.

name: KGB
description: The Committee for State Security of the USSR. Predecessor of the FSB.

name: Politsiya
description: Russian police force, the civilian law enforcement agency of the Russian Federation.

name: GUSP
description: Main Directorate for Special Programmes (Glavnoye Upravleniye Spetsialnykh Programm). Formerly the 15th Directorate of the KGB. An ultra-secret Russian security service that monitors Moscow's underground D-6 network. Guards wear a winged-shield insignia on their uniforms.

name: Ossa and Pelion
description: Tour company organizing the horticultural tour to French Riviera gardens including Les Pivoines. Group of eight members plus tour leader Pippa and expert lecturer Dan Melbury. Departing from St Pancras via Eurostar.

name: DGSI
description: French domestic intelligence service (Direction Generale de la Securite Interieure).

name: Scheubner-Richter Pharmaceuticals
description: Pharmaceutical company based in Oakland, California. Manufactures Gonzadril, a selective neurotransmitter and sexual-receptor agonist intended for libido stimulation.

name: GRU
description: Russian military intelligence service. Senior officers Borzhko and Peskov attempted to abduct Valentin Dominik at a Mayfair club, possibly connected to Russian elections and Operation Sanctify.

name: Buxor
description: Mining company owned by Majid Ismailov. Operates in Naziristan, extracting rare metals.

name: Alovert News Agency
description: Central Asian press agency covering Naziristan. Reports on Buxor mining company activities and human rights abuses in the region.
